learning_outcomes.description: At a high intermediate language level and in a culturally sensitive way discuss topics of interest through the use of social routines, exchange of information, ideas, opinions, experiences and plans.

At a high intermediate language level and in a culturally sensitive way participate in social interaction related to solving a problem, making arrangements and decisions, and transacting for services.
At a high intermediate language level and in a culturally sensitive way obtain information by searching for specific details in a spoken text, or by listening to or reading the text as a whole.
Understand the sociocultural aspects of the language they have learnt.
Learn to build up the attribute of persistence in doing something challenging like learning the target language.
Demonstrate respect for cultural and social diversity and for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and perspectives.
Demonstrate knowledge and respect for ethics and ethical standards in completing assessment tasks.
Lead self and others in group activities.
Be confident dealing with ambiguity and uncertainty.
Present persuasive oral and written arguments.
Be empathetic of other people, cultures, and knowledge systems.

assessments.task_description:
The rapid development of generative AI tools, such as ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Copilot, has profoundly shaped how we work, communicate, and learn. In higher education—particularly in language learning—AI now provides instant, human-like interaction opportunities that were previously difficult to access. Through this integration, students can develop the autonomy to treat AI as a supportive learning partner that enhances communication practice, stimulates interactive engagement, and extends learning beyond the traditional classroom. Nevertheless, while generative AI facilitates flexible and personalised learning, pedagogical interventions from teachers and authentic interactions with native speakers remain indispensable. This is because AI models are still prone to factual or linguistic errors, and genuine human interaction remains a critical cornerstone for developing true socio-cultural and communicative competence in a target language.

Task details: Each week, you will independently listen to a Chinese audio recording and engage in an interactive clarification/discussion session. You have the flexibility to complete this task using either a Generative AI tool (The UQ recommended AI tool Copilot) OR working with a native Chinese speaker of your choice. 

Step-by-Step Workflow:

  1. Independent Listening: Listen to the weekly audio file independently 1–3 times. As you listen, note down the overall story content, or the specific vocabulary, tones, and structures that you do not fully understand.
  2. Inquiry & Clarification (3–5 Questions):
  • If using AI: Upload the audio file to the AI tool. Use the provided prompts to initialise the conversation, ensuring the interaction remains entirely in Chinese. Ask the AI 3–5 targeted questions to clarify your problem areas.
  • If working with a Native Speaker: After listening to the audio file together, ask them 3–5 questions regarding the overall story content, or the specific vocabulary, tones, and structures you struggled with.

3.Comprehension Check (3–5 Questions): Once you feel you completely understand the recording, signal your partner (AI or native speaker) that you are ready. Have them ask you 3–5 comprehension questions based on the audio text. You must answer these questions entirely in Chinese. 

Submission & Deadlines:

  • Timeline: The listening audio will be released to everyone on Monday morning during Weeks 1–12.
  • Deadline: Your interaction recording must be uploaded to Blackboard/Ultra by 5:00 PM on the following Monday.
  • Evidence: You must record your interaction (either via audio, screen-recording, or video recording) showing both your questions and the comprehension check phase.

 Weekly Schedule & Story Titles will be provided in class. 

Marks are deducted only when:

(1) No recorded communication is evident; (2) Recordings are too short (fail to ask at least 3 questions or fail to answer all of the at least 3 questions from your native speaker language partner or AI tool.); (3) Submission of recording is too late; and (4) Recordings are not intelligible.

Statement on Generative AI & MT

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
In the exam, students are tested on their listening comprehension, knowledge of grammar and word usage and their ability to use language in a culturally appropriate manner. Questions in the exams will include items such as close exercises, correcting erroneous sentences, rearranging word order, multiple choice questions, dialogue composition, Chinese-English and English-Chinese Translations, and making sentences with key words. Testing of sentence structures is assessed according to the accuracy of meaning and grammar usage and contextual appropriateness of the structures and vocabulary used. Students' ability to comprehend the gist and details of a conversation and/or a report / speech in Chinese is assessed. Listening materials are mainly based on the texts & exercises covered. Questions types include filling in blanks, multiple choice and answering questions. The length is roughly about 80 minutes.

Statement on Generative AI & MT

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: A paper-based exam will be held on Wednesday WEEK 7 in the Tutorial. No dictionaries allowed.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
You are required to give a speech in Chinese in front of your classmates. You are expected to talk about what you have gained in learning Chinese. For example, you can talk about similarities and differences in Chinese and English or in Chinese and your mother tongue of your mother tongue is not English. You can talk about any cultural aspect you find interesting in Chinese or any experiences you would not have if you had not studied Chinese. Or you can discuss how you hope to use these language skills in the future, and how will the language skills help you to realise your dreams, particularly how the learning of Chinese helps you form friendship with native speakers of Chinese or anyone else, how the learning of Chinese helps you become more open-minded and more understanding of the world we live in, etc. The length of your oral presentation is around 3 minutes.

Specific topics: 1. What I have gained in learning Chinese; 2. How I see Chinese and English; 3. My Chinese friend and her/his strange habit; 4. How my Chinese language skills will help realize my dream.  5. Let me introduce the Chinese Bridge for you. Choose one of the topics listed or self-nominate a topic that is related to the Chinese language and culture for your oral presentation.  

Text submission: You will need to submit the text in pinyin and/or characters by the time or before you give your talk. You can consult your language partner, but s/he will only give you assistance with grammar points and word usage you are unsure of. You are the one who will draft the speech, not anyone else.  

The following aids are permitted during presentation:

1. One PPT slide is allowed, which is encouraged.

2. Palm cards can be used for occasional assistance. You are not allowed to simply read from it. If you do, it will be considered a reading, not a presentation, and you will gain a maximum mark of 50% only.

Criteria & Marking:

An overall assessment will be made that takes into account all of the following aspects of the presentation. 

  • Accuracy of pronunciation and tones
  • Content 
  • Accuracy of grammar 
  • Fluency 
  • Presentation techniques (clear, loud voice, interaction with audience etc)
  • Originality/Flair 

Statement on Generative AI & MT

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.task_description:
The final oral interview includes two parts: (1) Part 1 is a spontaneous dialogue between you and one of the examiners; (2) Part 2 consists of 10 questions to be answered by you that can be prepared in advance. Assessment is based on the accuracy of pronunciation and tones, content, accuracy of grammar, fluency, originality, range of vocabulary and structures, and cultural and situational appropriateness of language used. The total interview time for each student is about 20 minutes.

Statement on Generative AI & MT

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
